item.title: Wise shares slump over money laundering investigation
item.description: London-headquartered fintech says it is responding to questions from Belgian authorities

item.title: Chinese firms’ market share gains driven by subsidies, says OECD
item.description: Companies in China benefit from eight times more state support on average than rich-world rivals, report claims

item.title: UK house prices fall in May amid Iran turmoil
item.description: Higher mortgage rates blamed for Nationwide index’s first monthly decline this year

item.title: EasyJet says takeover bid would be ‘highly opportunistic’ as shares jump
item.description: US private credit group Castlelake says it is considering an approach for low-cost carrier

item.title: French private equity group Ardian backs €5bn AI ‘gigafactory’ outside Paris
item.description: Data centre and research facility to be built as Europe seeks to create ‘digital backbone for the future’

item.title: AI and real GDP: how big and how fast?
item.description: Plus more on the chip cycle

item.title: Saikat Chakrabarti is a leftist running for Congress. So what’s in his nine-figure portfolio?
item.description: Avowed progressive rejects the Golden Age of Private Credit

item.title: Nvidia unveils PC ‘superchip’ in challenge to Apple and Intel
item.description: Semiconductor paired with Microsoft Windows will allow computers to run AI apps

item.title: FirstFT: Intel to challenge Nvidia with AI chip
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Roche reinvention and Russian LNG

item.title: UK warns it could take action over crown dependencies’ lack of transparency
item.description: Government in fresh bid to persuade likes of Guernsey as well as overseas territories to go further in tackling illicit finance

item.title: FTSE 100’s likely new entrant puts a British spin on the AI boom
item.description: Hardware reseller’s trick will be to convince investors artificial intelligence can augment its services rather than replace them

item.title: US tax adviser Ryan strikes $400mn deal to take on Big Four in Europe
item.description: Texas-based group agrees to buy Svalner Atlas, which had been expected to attract bids from private equity firms

item.title: ‘More harmful than helpful’: young people sour on AI
item.description: Gen Z uses the technology more than anyone, but many fear it is weakening their job prospects and creativity

item.title: Danish shipyard still servicing LNG tankers for Russia trade
item.description: Fayard is only remaining EU yard taking on dry-dock work for ships carrying gas exported from Siberia

item.title: The truth about the American profit machine
item.description: It’s not much stronger now than it was during the dotcom boom

item.title: Global asset managers capture just 0.1% of Chinese market in 5 years
item.description: Foreign firms that launched their own businesses after Beijing relaxed ownership rules have attracted only $5bn

item.title: Is this the ultimate spin on the record deck?
item.description: Vinyl that floats on air – and four more gadgets for analogue fans

item.title: Can the company that shaped cancer treatment reinvent itself?
item.description: Roche dominated oncology a decade ago. Today it is struggling to keep up with its rivals

item.title: The Honeywell lifer deconstructing a 141-year-old industrials empire
item.description: Vimal Kapur is presiding over a three-way split that will usher in the end of corporate America’s conglomerate era

item.title: Europe is running from a phantom China threat
item.description: EU leaders fear cheap Chinese exports when they should be worrying about embedded spyware and rare earths dependency

item.title: Rival stouts try to persuade British drinkers to look beyond Guinness
item.description: UK’s appetite for the Irish stout continues to rise, particularly among young people

item.title: Xi’s last frontier: China’s plan to transform its west
item.description: A vast development drive aims to tap the region’s economic potential and extend Beijing’s control

item.title: Intel looks to level up in AI race
item.description: Leader of data centre unit says company aims to release a new graphics processing unit as shares rally more than 200% this year

item.title: Intel targets new AI data centre chip by year end
item.description: Leader of data centre unit says company aims to release ‘inference’ GPU as shares rally more than 200% this year

item.title: We need lifetime bans for clean athletes. This is the steroid Olympics, remember
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: SoftBank overtakes Toyota to become Japan’s largest company
item.description: Demand for AI stocks powers tech giant’s shares as it takes top spot by market capitalisation

item.title: Singapore tries to keep Asia’s oil flowing
item.description: City-state is conduit for big share of regional energy cargoes

item.title: Violence mars PSG celebrations after Champions League victory over Arsenal
item.description: French police make 780 arrests after Qatar-owned Paris Saint-Germain win trophy for second year running

item.title: Berkshire buys homebuilder Taylor Morrison for $8.5bn in Abel’s first big deal
item.description: First substantial acquisition under new CEO is bet on eventual recovery in property sector

item.title: Ritz-Carlton Yacht lenders ease terms to keep luxury cruise line afloat
item.description: Owners of troubled travel group inject $275mn in return for creditors delaying payment dates and relaxing terms
